ops.title: User rejects surgical multi-phase patch plans in cookiesync/synckit — wants one holistic redesign, unified call sites, better abstractions, root fixes pushed down into synckit
ops.body:
Rejecting a 7-phase surgical fix plan for the key-cache/consent bugs, the user said: "these are all bandaids, fold all of this into a proper holistic change, unify callsites, build better abstractions, fix the problem in synckit if possible."

**Why:** cookiesync/synckit is the user's own architecture; they prefer paying refactor cost once over accreting patch layers. A plan that fixes N call sites individually (even correctly) reads as bandaids; the same bugs should instead dissolve as a class under one abstraction (e.g. a single key-broker owning grant-check → cache → release → routing, one presence model instead of parallel locked sentinels, degradation as first-class state inside one cache type rather than a wrapper split). Generic plumbing (ssh transport, tunnel lifecycle, dispatch observability, possibly consent routing) belongs in the shared synckit substrate, not tool-local.

**How to apply:** when planning cookiesync/synckit fixes, lead with the unifying abstraction and the synckit-level root fix; per-call-site guards, wrapper shims, and message rewording are acceptable only as consequences of the redesign, never as the plan's spine. Related: [[cookiesync-architecture]], [[cookiesync-union-hang-and-deadline-fixes]].
